index.meta.title: Keeply — Your files have stories. Keeply remembers them all.
index.meta.description: The simplest way for non-technical users to manage file versions. Free download, start in 30 seconds.
index.hero.badge: Windows · macOS Desktop App · Free Download
index.hero.subtitle: Every edit, every decision, every note you leave becomes your project's memory. Six months from now, these memories will save you.
index.hero.cta.note: Start in 30 seconds, no account needed
index.hero.mock.today.note: Client requested cover design changes — switched to brand colors
index.hero.mock.yesterday.note: Updated cover colors and page layout
index.features.title: Every note. Every version. Always findable.
index.features.subtitle: More than a backup tool. Keeply protects every moment of your work.
index.features.1.title: Remember your WHY
index.features.1.desc: Don't just remember what changed — remember why you changed it.
index.features.1.detail: Six months later when a client asks "Who made this decision?" you won't need to dig through emails. Open Keeply — the answer is right there.
index.features.2.title: Your project time machine
index.features.2.desc: Every save is like taking a snapshot. Go back to any day, anytime.
index.features.2.detail: Made a mistake? Just go back. Client changed their mind? Find the old version in three seconds. Audit time? Complete records right here.
index.features.3.title: Edit boldly, we remember everything
index.features.3.desc: No more choosing between "make it better" and "too scared to change it."
index.features.3.detail: Keeply protects every version so you can experiment freely. No matter what you change, you can always go back.
index.usecases.title: No matter who you are, critical information needs to be remembered.
index.usecases.pm.title: Project Management
index.usecases.pm.desc: Every file in your project has a story. A single note from six months ago could save the entire project.
index.usecases.design.title: Design & Creative
index.usecases.design.desc: When a client says "Actually, I liked the first version better" — you need just three seconds. Every version is there.
index.usecases.legal.title: Legal & Contracts
index.usecases.legal.desc: Seventeen revisions of a contract, and every single one is preserved. Who changed it, when, and why — crystal clear.
index.usecases.academic.title: Academic Research
index.usecases.academic.desc: Thesis_final_REALLY_final_v3.docx? Track with Keeply and never get confused again.
index.usecases.construction.title: Construction & AEC
index.usecases.construction.desc: Site engineer grabs a USB drive for the client meeting. Admin approves the backup location — and revokes it when the project ends.
index.usecases.remote.title: Remote & Hybrid Teams
index.usecases.remote.desc: Office team on the NAS, remote designer on GitHub. Same vault, same version history — everyone stays in sync.
index.howto.title: Three steps to start, zero barriers.
index.howto.subtitle: No account needed, no server needed, no technical knowledge needed.
index.howto.step1.title: Download & Install
index.howto.step1.desc: Done in 30 seconds. Supports Windows 10+ and macOS.
index.howto.step2.title: Pick a folder
index.howto.step2.desc: Any folder you work in — blueprints, contracts, design files.
index.howto.step3.title: Start protecting
index.howto.step3.desc: From now on, every change is remembered. Want to go back? One click.

privacy.meta.title: Privacy Policy — Keeply
privacy.meta.description: Keeply is a local-first desktop application. Your files never leave your computer. Learn how we handle your data.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.highlight: Keeply is a local-first desktop application — your files never leave your computer. This privacy policy explains the minimal data we actually process, who handles it, and your rights.
privacy.toc.title: Table of Contents
privacy.toc.files: Your files stay on your device
privacy.files.title: Your files stay on your device
privacy.files.p1: Keeply manages version history of your project files locally. All project files are stored only on your computer or NAS. Keeply does not upload, transmit, access, read, or process your file contents. We do not operate any cloud storage servers and cannot view your work at any time.
privacy.files.p2: Network communications made by the Keeply desktop application are limited to:
privacy.files.li2: Update checks: Checks whether a new version is available
privacy.files.p3: The desktop application does not collect or transmit any telemetry, usage analytics, file metadata, or file contents.
privacy.notcollect.title: What we do NOT collect
privacy.notcollect.r1.cat: Your project files or file contents
privacy.notcollect.r2.cat: File names, paths, or directory structure
privacy.notcollect.r3.cat: Screen recordings, screenshots, or UI activity
privacy.notcollect.r4.cat: Credit card numbers or payment details
privacy.license.title: Data processed during license verification
privacy.license.p1: To verify your software license, Keeply communicates with Keygen.sh (Keygen LLC, Virginia, USA). The data transmitted includes:
privacy.license.li1: License key — Confirms whether the license is valid
privacy.license.li2: Vault location identifier — A one-way hash of your project vault's unique ID (cannot be traced back to the original identifier or your file contents)
privacy.license.li3: Application version — Checks compatibility and license scope
privacy.license.li4: Vault location token — A hashed identifier of your team's vault location (cannot be reversed to the actual storage path)
privacy.license.p2: We do not transmit your name, email address, or any file contents. Keygen holds SOC 2 Type II certification, uses Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) for international data transfers, and retains server logs for up to 30 days and analytics data for up to 90 days.
privacy.license.p3: For details, see the Keygen.sh Privacy Policy.
privacy.payment.title: Data processed during payment
privacy.payment.p1: All purchases of Keeply are processed by Paddle.com, which serves as our Merchant of Record. Your transaction is with Paddle, not Keeply. Paddle collects and processes: name, email address, billing address, payment method, location, and IP address.
privacy.payment.p2: Keeply never receives, stores, or accesses your credit card number or full payment details. Paddle may share limited buyer information (name, email, and transaction details) with us solely for order fulfillment and product support.
privacy.payment.p3: For details, see the Paddle Privacy Policy and Paddle Buyer Terms.
privacy.payment.p4: Legal basis (GDPR): Contractual necessity (Article 6(1)(b)) and legitimate interest in fraud prevention (Article 6(1)(f)).
privacy.website.title: Data collected on our website
privacy.website.p1: The Keeply marketing website (keeply.work) detects your preferred language entirely within your browser — we do not send your IP address to any third party for this purpose. Your language choice is saved to your browser's local storage only after you explicitly pick one from the language menu, or open a language-specific link (e.g. keeply.work/de/).
privacy.website.p2: If you contact us by email, we retain the correspondence to respond to your inquiry. You will not be added to any marketing list unless you explicitly opt in.
privacy.thirdparty.title: Third-party services
privacy.thirdparty.keygen.data: License key, vault location identifier (hash), version
privacy.thirdparty.paddle.data: Name, email, billing address, payment method
privacy.transfer.title: International data transfers
privacy.transfer.p1: Keeply is headquartered in Taiwan. Limited data may be transferred to the USA (Keygen.sh) and UK/USA (Paddle), governed by Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) and each party's GDPR compliance programs.
privacy.retention.title: Data retention
privacy.retention.r1.period: Up to 30 days (logs); up to 90 days (analytics)
privacy.retention.r2.period: As required by tax and financial regulations
privacy.retention.r3.period: Duration of business relationship, or upon deletion request
privacy.rights.title: Your rights
privacy.rights.p1: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, objection, and withdrawal of consent.
privacy.rights.gdpr: EU/EEA residents (GDPR): You have all of the above rights and may also lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority.
privacy.rights.ccpa: California residents (CCPA/CPRA): You have the right to know what personal information is collected, request deletion, and opt out. We do not sell personal information.
privacy.rights.taiwan: Taiwan residents (PDPA): You have the right to access, correct, and request deletion of your personal data.
privacy.rights.contact: Contact: hello@null.keeply.work — we will respond within 30 days.
privacy.eurep.title: EU Representative
privacy.eurep.p1: For the purposes of GDPR Article 27, our EU representative information is pending.
privacy.children.title: Children's privacy
privacy.children.p1: Keeply is not directed at children under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children.
privacy.changes.title: Changes to this policy
privacy.changes.p1: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Material changes will be posted on this website.
privacy.contactus.title: Contact Us
privacy.company: Keeply is a product of City-State Information Technology Co., Ltd. (城國資訊有限公司), registered in Taiwan (No. 42846493).
terms.meta.title: Terms of Service — Keeply
terms.meta.description: Keeply desktop application Terms of Service. Learn about licensing plans, payment policies, and your rights.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.highlight: These Terms of Service ("Agreement") govern your use of the Keeply desktop application ("Software"). By downloading, installing, or using the Software, you agree to be bound by this Agreement. If you do not agree, do not use the Software.
terms.toc.title: Table of Contents
terms.s1.title: 1. The Software
terms.s1.p1: Keeply is a desktop application that provides visual version control for project files. It runs locally on your computer and stores all project data on your local device or NAS. Keeply does not operate a user account service — software activation is handled through a License Key mechanism, with the license bound to devices you explicitly designate.
terms.s2.title: 2. License Grant
terms.s2.p1: The Software is licensed, not sold. Keeply grants you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable, revocable license to install and use the Software on any computer where you are the primary user, solely for your internal business or personal purposes.
terms.s2.p2: This Agreement does not convey any ownership, regardless of the use of terms such as "purchase," "sell," or "buy" on our website, in communications, or in third-party checkout flows.
terms.s3.title: 3. Licensing Plans
terms.s3.1.title: 3.1 Free Plan
terms.s3.1.p1: The Free plan provides full version control functionality, unlimited users, free forever. The Free plan does not include team management features (such as member management, permission controls, team activity logs). The Free plan is provided "as is" without service level commitments or guaranteed technical support.
terms.s3.1.p2: Keeply reserves the right to modify the features or availability of the Free plan at any time.
terms.s3.2.title: 3.2 Team Subscription Plan
terms.s3.2.p1: The Team plan uses flat-rate pricing (not per-seat):
terms.s3.2.p2: One price, no per-seat charges. Includes all Free plan features plus: member management & permissions, team activity log, priority technical support.
terms.s3.2.p3: Subscriptions auto-renew at the end of each billing cycle unless canceled. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing cycle — you can continue using the service until then. Subscription fees are non-refundable except as described in Section 4.
terms.s3.3.title: 3.3 Perpetual License (Early Access Limited Offer)
terms.s3.3.p1: A perpetual license is available at $599 as an Early Access limited offer, limited to 500 copies. One-time payment, use forever.
terms.s3.3.note.title: Key details:
terms.s3.3.note.li1: Includes all feature updates and bug fixes within the major version at time of purchase (e.g. v1.x)
terms.s3.3.note.li2: Does not include upgrades to the next major version (e.g. v2.0). Existing perpetual license holders get a 40% discount on the next major version upgrade
terms.s3.3.note.li3: Keeply commits to maintaining each major version for at least 18 months from its initial release. v2.0 will not be released earlier than 18 months after v1.0 launch
terms.s3.3.note.li4: Keeply reserves the right to discontinue or modify this offer at any time
terms.s3.3.note.li5: Perpetual licenses purchased before discontinuation remain valid under their original terms
terms.s3.3.note.li6: Early access to new features and exclusive community channel
terms.s3.4.title: 3.4 Perpetual Fallback License (Safety Net After One Year)
terms.s3.4.p1: After subscribing to the Team plan for 12 consecutive months, if you choose to cancel, you may permanently use the version available on the day of cancellation (Perpetual Fallback License). After cancellation, you will not receive any further updates (including feature updates and bug fixes). To get the latest features, simply resubscribe anytime.
terms.s3.4.p2: Condition: 12 consecutive months of subscription without interruption or outstanding payments. If cancelled or interrupted mid-cycle, the 12-month accumulation period resets.
terms.s3.4.example.title: Example:
terms.s3.4.example.desc: You start Team annual billing in January 2027. You cancel in March 2028, when Keeply is at v2.4.1.
terms.s3.4.example.yes1: ✅ After canceling, your team continues using v2.4.1, free forever
terms.s3.4.example.no1: ❌ You won't receive v2.5, v3.0, or future versions
terms.s3.4.example.yes2: ✅ When you want new versions, just re-subscribe
terms.s3.4.exclude.title: When this does not apply:
terms.s3.4.exclude.li1: Canceled before 12 months → Access ends at current billing cycle
terms.s3.4.exclude.li2: Interrupted or outstanding payments → 12-month clock resets
terms.s3.4.exclude.li3: Terminated for violating this Agreement → Does not apply
terms.s3.4.compare.r1.label: How to get it
terms.s3.4.compare.r2.label: What you get
terms.s3.4.compare.r2.perpetual: All updates within the major version at time of purchase (e.g. v1.x)
terms.s3.4.compare.r2.fallback: Permanently use the version at time of cancellation
terms.s3.4.compare.r3.label: Future updates?
terms.s3.4.compare.r3.perpetual: Free updates within the same major version
terms.s3.4.compare.r3.fallback: No, frozen at the version on cancellation day
terms.s3.4.compare.r4.label: Best for
terms.s3.4.compare.r4.perpetual: Teams wanting a one-time purchase for long-term use
terms.s3.4.compare.r4.fallback: Teams wanting to try subscribing first and keep usage rights later
terms.s3.4.compare.r5.label: Availability
terms.s4.title: 4. Payment, Refunds & Paddle
terms.s4.1.title: 4.1 Merchant of Record
terms.s4.1.p1: Our checkout process is operated by Paddle.com. Paddle is the Merchant of Record for all our orders, handling payment processing, invoicing, tax calculation and remittance, and billing-related customer support.
terms.s4.1.p2: When you purchase a license, your transaction is with Paddle, not directly with Keeply. The Software is licensed to you by Keeply; it is sold to you by Paddle.
terms.s4.1.p3: Your purchase is also subject to Paddle Buyer Terms and Paddle Refund Policy.
terms.s4.2.title: 4.2 Refund Policy
terms.s4.2.highlight: All paid plans (including Team plan and perpetual license) come with a 30-day money-back guarantee — contact hello@null.keeply.work or request through Paddle within 30 days of purchase for a full refund. License is revoked upon refund.
terms.s4.2.p1: After 30 days, refunds are handled according to Paddle's refund policy and applicable consumer protection laws. EU/EEA/UK residents have a 14-day statutory right of withdrawal; Taiwan residents may have a 7-day inspection period under consumer protection law.
terms.s4.2.p2: For billing inquiries and refunds, contact Paddle (paddle.com/contact) or hello@null.keeply.work.
terms.s4.3.title: 4.3 Price Changes
terms.s4.3.p1: We may change pricing with at least 30 days' written notice before the change takes effect. Price changes apply at the next renewal.
terms.s4.4.title: 4.4 Taxes
terms.s4.4.p1: Paddle as MoR is responsible for calculating, collecting, and remitting all applicable sales tax, VAT, and GST.
terms.s5.title: 5. Use Restrictions
terms.s5.li1: Reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive the source code, unless expressly permitted by applicable law
terms.s5.li2: Copy, distribute, sublicense, rent, lease, or lend the Software or license keys
terms.s5.li3: Modify, adapt, translate, or create derivative works
terms.s5.li4: Remove or alter any proprietary notices
terms.s5.li5: Circumvent or disable any license verification or security features
terms.s5.li6: Publicly share or publish license keys
terms.s5.li7: Use the Software to provide hosting services to third parties
terms.s5.li8: Use the Software for any illegal purpose
terms.s6.title: 6. Intellectual Property
terms.s6.p1: The Software and all copies, modifications, and derivative works are the exclusive property of Keeply and its licensors. The Software includes open-source components licensed under their respective licenses.
terms.s7.title: 7. Data Privacy and Activity Logs
terms.s8.title: 8. Warranty Disclaimer
terms.s8.p1: The Software is provided "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" without warranties of any kind, including but not limited to implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, title, and non-infringement.
terms.s8.p2: Data & File Disclaimer: The Software interacts with your local files and storage devices. You are solely responsible for maintaining adequate backups of all files and data. Keeply is not liable for any file loss or corruption arising from use of the Software.
terms.s9.title: 9. Limitation of Liability
terms.s9.p1: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law: Keeply shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages. Keeply's total cumulative liability shall not exceed the amount you actually paid in the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the claim, or $100, whichever is greater.
terms.s10.title: 10. Term & Termination
terms.s10.p1: This Agreement takes effect from your first use of the Software and continues until terminated. You may terminate this Agreement at any time by deactivating the software license and removing all local Keeply data (including but not limited to project repositories, activity logs, shadow backups, and license files). Deactivation methods include: (a) using the "Deactivate License and Remove Local Data" function within the Keeply application; or (b) uninstalling Keeply from your system and manually deleting the local Keeply data directory.
terms.s10.p2: We may revoke your license if you breach this Agreement. Upon termination, Perpetual License holders and Perpetual Fallback License holders retain the right to use their respective versions, but will no longer receive updates.
terms.s11.title: 11. Governing Law & Disputes
terms.s11.p1: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of China (Taiwan), excluding CISG. Disputes shall first be negotiated for 30 days; if unresolved, submitted to the Taipei District Court, Taiwan. This section does not limit your statutory rights under consumer protection laws.
terms.s12.title: 12. General Provisions
terms.s12.p1: Entire Agreement. This Agreement, together with the Privacy Policy and Paddle Buyer Terms, constitutes the entire agreement.
terms.s12.p2: Severability. If any provision is unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain in effect.
terms.s12.p3: Assignment. You may not assign this Agreement. Keeply may assign in the event of a merger, acquisition, or asset sale.
terms.s12.p4: Amendments. Material changes will be notified 30 days in advance. Users on fallback versions may continue under prior terms.
terms.s12.p5: Language. This Agreement is drafted in English; in case of conflict with translations, the English version prevails.
terms.s13.title: 13. Contact
terms.company: Keeply is a product of City-State Information Technology Co., Ltd. (城國資訊有限公司), registered in Taiwan (No. 42846493).
terms.s3.5.title: 3.5 Definitions
terms.s3.5.p1: "Team Vault" means a shared storage location — including but not limited to a Network Attached Storage (NAS) device, file server, self-hosted Git server, or cloud-hosted Git remote repository — designated as the central collaboration point. A Team Vault is identified by a unique location token generated by the Software and stored within the repository.
terms.s3.5.p2: "Vault Location" means the unique identifier associated with a Team Vault, derived from the repository's embedded location token and/or the normalized Git remote URL. Vault Locations are classified into two types:
terms.s3.5.p2a: (a) "Fixed Location" means a Vault Location set by the team admin and shared by all team members, up to a maximum of 2;
terms.s3.5.p2b: (b) "Flexible Location" means a Vault Location requested by a team member and approved by the admin for that member's personal use, up to 1 per member.
terms.s3.6.title: 3.6 Vault Location Rules
terms.s3.6.p1: Changes to Fixed Locations are subject to the following:
terms.s3.6.li1: (a) A cool-down period of seven (7) days from the most recent change;
terms.s3.6.li2: (b) No more than 6 changes per year;
terms.s3.6.li3: (c) Migration of repository data (including the embedded location token) to new storage hardware does not constitute a Fixed Location change, provided the location token remains unchanged.
terms.s3.6.p2: Flexible Locations are requested by members through the Software and take effect upon admin approval. The admin may revoke any member's Flexible Location at any time. Flexible Locations not accessed for 30 consecutive days are automatically released.
terms.s3.7.title: 3.7 License Scope
terms.s3.8.title: 3.8 Fair Use
terms.s3.8.p1: Keeply plans are designed for use by a single organization's team. "Organization" means a company or legal entity and its employees, consultants, and contractors. Users shall not: (a) Share license keys or credentials with unauthorized third parties; (b) Artificially circumvent Vault Location limits by routing multiple independent teams through a single storage location; (c) Resell, sublicense, or redistribute access to the Software or any Vault.
terms.s3.8.p2: If we detect usage patterns significantly exceeding expected norms (e.g., team size exceeding 10× the plan average), we may contact you to discuss a more suitable plan. Our goal is to find the right fit, not to penalize growth.
contact.meta.title: Contact Us — Keeply
contact.meta.description: Have a question, want to report a bug, or just want to say hello? Contact the Keeply team.
contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.subtitle: Have a question, want to report a bug, or just want to say hello?
contact.email.title: Email
contact.email.response: We aim to respond within 2 business days.
contact.billing.title: Billing & Refunds
contact.billing.desc: All payments are processed by Paddle.com. For billing questions or refunds, contact hello@null.keeply.work or visit Paddle Support.
contact.billing.note: Team plan includes a 30-day money-back guarantee.
contact.security.title: Security & Privacy
contact.security.desc: Found a security vulnerability or have a privacy concern? Send an email to hello@null.keeply.work with "Security" or "Privacy" in the subject line.
contact.security.privacy: To learn how we handle your data, see our Privacy Policy.
contact.company: Keeply is a product of City-State Information Technology Co., Ltd. (城國資訊有限公司), registered in Taiwan (No. 42846493).
index.pricing.team.label: Team
index.pricing.team.coming: Currently in development. Leave your email to get notified when it launches.
index.pricing.team.newsletter: I'd also like to receive Keeply product updates and newsletter
index.pricing.team.thanks: Got it! We'll notify you when the Team plan launches.
index.pricing.perpetual.title: Perpetual License — $599 one-time purchase
index.pricing.perpetual.desc: Includes all Team features, use the version at purchase forever. While supplies last.
index.teamvault.tagline: One vault. One team. Full control.
index.teamvault.title: What is a "Team Vault"?
index.teamvault.desc: A "vault location" is where you store project versions — an office NAS, a cloud storage service, or a team member's personal backup drive. Keeply lets you freely combine these locations.
index.teamvault.highlight: No cloud lock-in. No per-seat fees. Your data stays on your own hardware.
index.teamvault.free: 3 vault locations, fully self-managed. No approval needed, no cooldowns.
index.teamvault.team: 2 shared team locations + 1 backup location per member (up to 15 total). Admin approval, cooldowns, and annual limits keep backup space organized.
refund.meta.title: Refund Policy — Keeply
refund.meta.description: Keeply Refund Policy. Learn about the 30-day money-back guarantee, perpetual license refund terms, and your statutory rights.
refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.highlight: This Refund Policy applies to all purchases of Keeply software made through our authorized payment processor, Paddle.com.
refund.toc.title: Table of Contents
refund.s1.title: 1. Merchant of Record
refund.s1.p1: All purchases are processed by Paddle.com, which acts as the Merchant of Record for all Keeply orders. Paddle is responsible for payment processing, invoicing, tax calculation, and billing-related customer support.
refund.s1.p2: When you purchase a Keeply license, you are transacting with Paddle, not directly with Keeply. Your purchase is also subject to Paddle's Buyer Terms and Paddle's own refund policies.
refund.s2.title: 2. 30-Day Money-Back Guarantee (Team Plan)
refund.s2.p1: Keeply's Team Plan (monthly and annual subscriptions) comes with a 30-day money-back guarantee.
refund.s2.p2: If you are not satisfied with your purchase for any reason, contact us within 30 days of your initial purchase date and we will issue a full refund — no questions asked.
refund.s2.li1: Email hello@null.keeply.work with your order details, or
refund.s2.li2: Contact Paddle directly at paddle.com/contact
refund.s3.title: 3. Early Access Perpetual License
refund.s3.p1: The Early Access Perpetual License ($599) is eligible for a refund within 30 days of purchase, provided the license key has not been activated or used.
refund.s3.p2: After 30 days, perpetual license purchases are non-refundable.
refund.s4.title: 4. After the 30-Day Window
refund.s4.p1: After 30 days, refunds are handled on a case-by-case basis in accordance with:
refund.s4.li2: Applicable consumer protection laws in your jurisdiction
refund.s4.sub.title: Statutory Rights
refund.s4.sub.li1: EU / EEA / UK residents: You may have a statutory 14-day right of withdrawal for digital products, subject to applicable conditions.
refund.s4.sub.li2: Taiwan residents: You may have rights under Taiwan's Consumer Protection Act (消費者保護法), including a potential 7-day inspection period.
refund.s4.p2: These statutory rights are not limited by this policy.
refund.s5.title: 5. Non-Refundable Items
refund.s5.p1: The following are generally not eligible for refunds:
refund.s5.li1: Subscription fees for periods already used beyond the 30-day window
refund.s5.li2: Renewals (monthly or annual) after the renewal has processed
refund.s5.li3: Perpetual licenses purchased more than 30 days ago
refund.s6.title: 6. How to Request a Refund
refund.s6.opt1.title: Option 1 — Contact Keeply:
refund.s6.opt1.li1: Your order ID or email address used at purchase
refund.s6.opt1.li2: The reason for your refund request
refund.s6.opt2.title: Option 2 — Contact Paddle directly:
refund.s6.opt2.desc: Visit paddle.com/contact or use the receipt email you received from Paddle.
refund.s6.p1: We aim to respond to all refund requests within 2 business days.
refund.s7.title: 7. Refund Processing

terms.s7.20_1.title: 20.1 Keeply Server-Side Commitment
terms.s7.20_1.html:
Your use of the Software is also governed by our Privacy Policy. Keeply's servers do not access, collect, store, or transmit any of the following:

  • Your project files (any files within working directories)
  • Project version snapshots (Git objects stored under .git by Keeply)
  • Activity logs (hash chain events stored in ~/.keeply/activity.db)
  • Backup location contents (any data under your designated USB / NAS / Gitea paths)
  • Shadow backup contents (snapshots under .git/keeply/shadow/)

All of the above exists only on your local device and locations you explicitly designate; none is ever transmitted to Keeply servers.
terms.s7.20_2.title: 20.2 Local Activity Log
terms.s7.20_2.html:
Keeply maintains a local activity log on your device at ~/.keeply/activity.db (SQLite database). This log is used for:

  • Cross-version traceability: reviewing past save, restore, and export events
  • Crash recovery: rebuilding working state after unexpected termination
  • Team plan audit features (only when Team plan is active)

The activity log records only event types and timestamps, never:

  • File contents
  • Full file path strings (only minimal relative paths needed for traceability)
  • Your in-editor operations
terms.s7.20_3.title: 20.3 Backup Location Activity Log Copy (Dual-write Behavior)
terms.s7.20_3.html:
When you configure a backup location (e.g., USB device, NAS, Docker Gitea instance), Keeply creates an activity log copy at {backup_path}/.keeply-activity/{machine_id}.jsonl during sync.

{machine_id} is a hashed device identifier (HMAC-SHA256 derived value) and does not contain your raw MAC address, drive serial, or other unprocessed device characteristics.

This copy serves the same purposes as the local activity log in §20.2 (cross-machine traceability / crash recovery / Team audit).

If your backup location is shared with others (e.g., company NAS, Docker Gitea organizational instance), persons with access to that location may observe this copy's existence and contents. If this is a concern, you can disable this behavior in "Settings → Activity Log → Backup Location Sync".

Disabling reduces cross-machine traceability capability, but the local activity log continues to operate.
terms.s7.20_4.title: 20.4 Keeply Server-Side Necessary Services
terms.s7.20_4.html:
While Keeply's servers do not receive your project or activity contents, the following functions involve server-side communication with Keeply or third-party services:

  • License validation (via Keygen.sh, USA): transmits hashed device identifiers to verify license validity
  • Payment processing (via Paddle, UK/Ireland): handles subscriptions, refunds, and billing matters
  • Update checks: periodic queries for new software versions

Details of these services' data processing are covered in our Privacy Policy.
terms.s3.7.15_1.title: 15.1 Core Restriction
terms.s3.7.15_1.html: Each license is granted to a single legal entity. This license may not be shared, pooled, or used for the benefit of unrelated third parties or separate legal entities.
terms.s3.7.15_2.title: 15.2 Permitted Uses (Exemptions)
terms.s3.7.15_2.html:
The following situations fall within the scope of single-legal-entity license permitted use:

  • (a) Employees and Contractors: employees, independent contractors, and agents of the licensed legal entity, to the extent they are providing services to that entity
  • (b) Affiliates: Affiliates of the licensed legal entity (as defined in §15.4) may share a single license, provided all use remains within the core restriction
  • (c) Individual Freelancers: an individual natural person licensee using the software while providing services to their multiple clients (the licensee remains the sole user; this shall not be deemed multi-entity sharing)
terms.s3.7.15_3.title: 15.3 Audit Rights
terms.s3.7.15_3.html:
Upon reasonable written notice, we reserve the right to request documentation from the licensee to verify license compliance, including:

  • Business registration documents (e.g., Taiwan Unified Business Number 統一編號, EU VAT ID, UK Companies House number, US EIN)
  • Authorized representative's name and title
  • In the event of reasonable suspicion of breach, explanation of the scope of licensed use

Absent reasonable suspicion of breach of this Agreement, we will not exercise audit rights more than once per twelve (12) months.

The existence of this clause does not constitute a waiver of any past non-exercise of audit rights.
terms.s3.7.15_4.title: 15.4 Affiliate Definition
terms.s3.7.15_4.html:
In this Agreement, "Affiliate" means:

  • An entity that directly or indirectly holds 50% or more of the voting shares / interests of the licensed legal entity
  • An entity of which the licensed legal entity directly or indirectly holds 50% or more of the voting shares / interests
  • An entity under common control with the licensed legal entity (i.e., common shareholders each hold 50% or more of voting shares in both entities)
